2. Explain what self-harm is and why people might do it.
3. Provide tips on how to recognize the warning signs of self-harm or suicidal thoughts in oneself or others.

5. Encourage children to be supportive and understanding of their peers who may be struggling with self-harm or suicidal thoughts.

Hello there! In this tutorial, we will be discussing a very sensitive topic - self-harm and suicide. It is important to approach this subject with care and sensitivity, and to remember that if you or someone you know is struggling with these issues, it is crucial to seek help from a qualified professional.

Self-harm refers to the act of intentionally harming oneself, while suicide is the act of intentionally ending one's own life. These behaviors can be complex and multifaceted, and there are many different factors that can contribute to them.
